new_feedback.element: Inspect drawer (INSPECT · SPEC VALIDATOR CALL) vs. the ERROR state
new_feedback.issue: There is a single shared Inspect drawer and its body is hard-coded to a successful validator call: RAW RESPONSE shows {"sealed":true,...,"verdict":"valid"} and PARSED OUTPUT reads 'valid · sealed · 3 obligations'. In the ERROR state the page itself reports PARSE ERROR at line 6 with 'no oracle obligations were registered.' If an operator inspects the validator call from the error route, the audit drawer asserts the call returned valid while the surface asserts it failed. For SR 11-7 traceability the inspected call must match the run's actual verdict; a drawer that always shows 'valid' makes the inspect surface unreliable as an audit record and would not survive examiner scrutiny.
new_feedback.change: Give the Inspect drawer an error variant bound to the validation attempt being inspected: in the error state it should render the validator's actual rejecting response (verdict: invalid, the YAMLParseError, line/column, 0 obligations registered) under the same model-identity and redaction-policy header. The drawer's verdict must equal the page's verdict for the same attempt id (a3f9c2).

new_feedback.element: Estimate drawer · SOURCE RUNS block ('median of last 12 fraud runs')
new_feedback.issue: The block is labelled as the median of the last 12 fraud runs but lists only three run IDs (r_7c1d $7.92, r_6b09 $8.41, r_5aa1 $9.10) with no indication the other nine are elided, and the stated median of those visible figures ($8.41) does not reconcile with the headline 'EST / RUN $8.40 / $8.40 floor'. A model risk officer auditing the cost basis sees a 12-run claim backed by 3 visible rows and a median that does not match the quoted estimate, which reads as the provenance not adding up.
new_feedback.change: Either show all 12 source-run rows (scrollable) or label the three as a sample with a '+9 more' affordance, and add one line tying the rows to the headline ('median $8.41 → floored to $8.40 per cost-model v2.1'), so the displayed estimate is reproducible from the displayed inputs.
